kopplung.interpretation: Emotional attunement is medium and one-sided — Person A reads Person B's state accurately, the other way around happens less. The rhythm is asymmetric: Person A keeps a fast reply cadence, Person B stretches reply times, especially on emotionally weighted topics. A shared lexical zone exists — borrowed phrasings, inside jokes — showing a shared micro-culture despite the structural imbalance.

machtstruktur.interpretation: Person A sets almost every topic and carries the emotional temperature of the chat. Person B steers the process — decides when to answer, when a topic ends, when there's a break. This split means: Person A produces content, Person B regulates the frame. Structural power outweighs topical initiative here.

zitate.text: I've been thinking a lot about us today
do we have to do this now?

bindungsdyade.dyaden_beschreibung: The classic insecure trap: Person A experiences connection through emotional opening, Person B regulates intimacy through distance. The more A seeks, the more B deactivates. The more B pulls back, the more A hyperactivates.
bindungsdyade.dyaden_risiko: A self-reinforcing loop: A reads distance as a threat and seeks more, B reads seeking as pressure and withdraws more. Without a conscious break, this tips into chronic overactivation in A and chronic stonewalling in B.
bindungsdyade.interpretation: The pattern is clearly visible — protest-style doubling down after silence from A, short deactivating replies from B, fast de-escalation to warmth from A after her own "too much". A chat isn't an attachment diagnostic, but the signature is consistent.

bid.text: so looking forward to this weekend with you

antwort.text: oh no tell me, what's up?

bids.interpretation: Person A invests heavily in bids — small attentions, shared details, vulnerability. Person B mostly turns away (one-word, topic change) and occasionally against (rejection). When B sends a rare bid, A turns strongly toward. The asymmetry is Gottman's strongest predictor of erosion: the toward/away ratio here is far below the 5:1 of stable couples.

repair.typische_form: Sweet note from A, often with an apology upfront — "sorry that was a lot". Rarely naming the break itself.
repair.interpretation: Repair exists, but it's asymmetric: A carries the repair load completely. B accepts most repair attempts silently, initiates none. Short-term this stabilizes; long-term it normalizes A always coming back — and lets B off the hook for the dyad. A resource imbalance over time.

zitate.text: sorry that was a lot, I love you

flooding_hinweise.hinweis: Abrupt brevity and hours of silence after emotional messages — classic flooding pattern.

konflikt_signatur.interpretation: Gottman's demand-withdraw polarity is clearly at play. A occasionally shows criticism ("you never do…") but mostly slides into worry and repair. B shows stonewalling (withdrawal, wall) and mild defensiveness (victim stance). Contempt — Gottman's strongest negative predictor — is not present. That's an important line.

zitate.text: you never talk to me when something's up
I don't have the energy for this

pro_person.beschreibung: Reads B's state in a nuanced way: distinguishes exhausted from annoyed, names her own projections ("am I being paranoid right now?").
Barely models A's inner world — reacts to behavior ("you write too much"), not to the affect behind it.

mentalisierung.projektion_statt_modellierung: Person B projects in several moments: "you want to control me" — with no evidence of controlling intent. That's the classic mentalization gap under stress.
mentalisierung.interpretation: The mentalization asymmetry amplifies the attachment asymmetry: A feels seen and at the same time overestimates how much B can track. B experiences A as "too intense" because B doesn't model A's inner emotional world, only evaluates behavior.

zitate.text: I think you're just totally overloaded from work

meta_kommunikation.blocker_muster: Person B deflects meta-talk about the relationship — with "let's not hash this out over chat", "too dramatic for me", or simply not replying until the topic evaporates.
meta_kommunikation.interpretation: The dyad can talk about daily life, logistics and topics fine, but not about itself. Every attempt from A to name the relationship gets pushed back onto the content level. Without a meta level, rules can never be negotiated — they just get enacted unconsciously.

zitate.text: can we talk about us for once, not about stuff?

ulterior_transactions.sozial: A asks a neutral question about weekend plans.
ulterior_transactions.psychologisch: A is testing whether B is thinking about A — the real wish is confirmation of the bond.

beispiel.text: what are you up to Saturday actually?

ulterior_transactions.sozial: B says "it's fine" after a conflict.
ulterior_transactions.psychologisch: B closes the topic without resolution — the message is "don't come closer with this".

ulterior_transactions.sozial: A sends a "sorry" after a longer message.
ulterior_transactions.psychologisch: A pre-apologizes for her own presence and invites B to refuse reassurance.

beispiel.text: sorry I'm rambling, ignore if annoying

games.name: I'm too much
games.funktion: A names herself as the problem, gets half-hearted reassurance from B, status quo holds. The game keeps B from having to own the dynamic.

beispiel.text: I know I'm being exhausting right now

berne.interpretation: The surface of the chat is friendly and everyday. Underneath, a psychological layer runs constantly, negotiating attachment confirmation without naming it. That's Berne's classic ulterior configuration — and the reason the chat feels calm, even though the core question is being worked out all the time without resolution.

regeln.regel: Person A makes the first move after every break.
regeln.funktion: Keeps the relationship moving without calling B to account.
regeln.evidenz: Every conflict in the sample ends with an opening message from A, never from B.

regeln.regel: We don't talk about the relationship itself, only about everyday stuff.
regeln.funktion: Protects B from emotional escalation, protects A from a dismissive response.
regeln.evidenz: Meta-talk attempts systematically evaporate. The longest threads are about logistics.

regeln.regel: Whoever raises their voice has lost.
regeln.funktion: Prevents open escalation — and with it, open clarification.
regeln.evidenz: No direct anger in the sample. Instead: withdrawal, apology, joke.

regeln.regel: A is in charge of warmth, B is in charge of pace.
regeln.funktion: A role distribution that relieves both short-term and cements both long-term.
regeln.evidenz: Sweet notes only from A, pace regulation only from B.

unausgesprochene_regeln.interpretation: The rules are functional — they keep the dyad alive. They are at the same time the reason it doesn't change: non-negotiable because never spoken. Whoever names an unspoken rule breaks it — that's the price of change.

kern_insight: This dyad works because it never talks about itself — and that is exactly what will keep it from ever changing.
